title: JWT Token Expiry Configuration Error Causing Mass User Logouts
symptoms: Mass user logout events across all platforms (web, mobile, API)
Authentication service returning 429 Too Many Requests
JWT token refresh rate spiking from 200/min to 45,000/min
User session counts dropping to near zero across all regions
Customer support queue flooded with login failure reports
root_cause: Configuration deployment error setting JWT access token TTL to 60 seconds instead of 3600 seconds, causing rapid token expiry and overwhelming the auth service with refresh requests
resolution: Rolled back the authentication service configuration to restore 3600-second token TTL, scaled up auth service replicas to handle the login surge, and cleared the token refresh queue
preventive_actions: Implement configuration change validation with bounds checking for critical values
Add canary deployment for authentication configuration changes
Create automated rollback trigger for abnormal token refresh rates
Implement rate limiting on token refresh endpoint per user
Add configuration diff review requirement for auth service changes

timeline.event: Configuration deployment to auth service with incorrect JWT TTL
First wave of token expiry events, users begin getting logged out
Token refresh rate spikes, auth service under heavy load
Auth service returns 429 errors, users unable to log in
P1 alert fires, Aisha Khan begins investigation
Configuration error identified, JWT TTL set to 60s
Configuration rolled back to previous version
Auth service scaled up, login functionality restored

embedding_summary: JWT token expiry misconfiguration in authentication service set TTL to 60 seconds instead of 3600 seconds, causing mass logout of 45,000 users. Auth service overwhelmed by token refresh storm. 15-minute login outage. Resolved by configuration rollback and auth service scaling.
